'''Andrew Scott Coley''' (born 7 July 1978) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}} and {{rlp|sr}} forward in the 1990s, 2000s and 2010s. A [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] international forward, he played in the [[Super League]] for the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] and the [[Wigan Warriors]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Career==
===Early career===
Born in [[Warrington]], Coley began playing rugby league for local amateur side Laporte ARL.<ref>{{cite web |title=Andy Coley Factfile |url=https://wiganwarriors.com/news/2007-09-08-andy-coley-factfile |publisher=Wigan Warriors |accessdate=30 May 2019 |date=8 September 2007}}</ref> 

===Swinton Lions===
He turned professional in 1997, joining the [[Swinton Lions]]. 

In March 2000, Coley suffered a cruciate ligament injury, which ruled him out for the rest of the season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Lions bid to bounce back |url=https://www.lancashiretelegraph.co.uk/news/6087929.lions-bid-bounce-back/ |website=Lancashire Telegraph |access-date=8 October 2022 |date=31 March 2000}}</ref>

===Salford City Reds===
In 2001, Coley signed for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] as a {{rlp|sr}} prior to the 2001's [[Super League VI]], but subsequently moved up into the front row. Coley impressed in his first season at the club, and was rewarded with a new four-year contract.<ref>{{cite news |title=Sporting Digest: Rugby League |url=https://infoweb.newsbank.com/apps/news/document-view?p=AWNB&docref=news/132A8CAD3AF15B00 |work=The Independent |date=8 May 2001 |page=23 |via=[[NewsBank]] |url-access=subscription}}</ref> He required another knee operation at the end of the year, which ruled him out of the first few months of the 2002 season.<ref>{{cite news |title=Coley faces lengthy layoff |url=https://infoweb.newsbank.com/apps/news/document-view?p=AWNB&docref=news/143E53A6EC7E84A8 |work=Salford Advertiser |date=19 December 2001 |via=[[NewsBank]] |url-access=subscription}}</ref>

In 2004, Coley signed a new three-year contract with Salford.<ref>{{cite web |title=Coley sticks with Reds |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/salford/3878213.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=9 October 2022 |date=8 July 2004}}</ref>

Coley agreed a deal to join [[Wigan Warriors]] in September 2007 from [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] who were relegated in the 2007 [[Super League]] season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Wigan snap up Coley from Salford |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wigan/6981878.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=9 October 2022 |date=6 September 2007}}</ref>

===Wigan Warriors===
Coley was first team {{rlp|pr}} at the [[Wigan Warriors]] since his arrival.

He played in the [[2010 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Wilson|first=Andy|title=Wigan turn St Helens into sinners with Super League Grand Final win|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/oct/02/wigan-st-helens-grand-final|accessdate=21 November 2019|newspaper=guardian.co.uk|date=2 October 2010}}</ref>

Coley played as a {{rlp|pr}} forward in the [[2011 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Scott|first=Ged|title=Leeds 18-28 Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14670204|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=[[BBC Sport]]|date=27 August 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite news| url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/aug/27/leeds-rhinos-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup | location=London | work=The Guardian | first=Andy | last=Wilson | title=Leeds Rhinos 18-28 Wigan Warriors – Challenge Cup final match report | date=27 August 2011}}</ref> Shortly before the final, Coley announced he would be retiring from rugby league at the end of the 2011 season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Wigan prop Andy Coley to retire at end of season |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14617108 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=9 October 2022 |date=22 August 2011}}</ref>

===Representative career===
Coley's first representative honours came in 2001 when he represented [[Lancashire rugby league team|Lancashire]].

In 2004, he was selected for England during their victorious European Nations Cup campaign, scoring a hat trick of tries on his England début against Russia.

In 2006, he received his first call-up for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] when he was selected for the [[2006 Rugby League Tri-Nations|2006 Tri Nations]]. He appeared as a substitute in a warm-up game against [[Newcastle rugby league team|Newcastle]],<ref>{{cite web |title=Peacock avoids warm-up red |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2006/oct/21/rugbyleague.gdnsport3 |website=The Guardian |access-date=9 October 2022 |date=21 October 2006}}</ref> but did not feature in any Test matches during the tournament. In June 2007, Coley won his first and only cap for Great Britain in a 42–14 win against [[France national rugby league team|France]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Great Britain 42-14 France |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/6228054.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=9 October 2022 |date=22 June 2007}}</ref>

==Post-playing career==
Coley is a qualified [[quantity surveyor]], and became Operations Director of a company in the construction industry.<ref>{{cite web |title=Our People |url=https://www.greenmountprojects.co.uk/about-us/our-people/ |publisher=Greenmount Projects |access-date=9 October 2022}}</ref>
